Appearances on Wednesday, December 10, 2014:
Brian K. Hilliard of The Hilliard Firm (510.653.8886) attorney for Plaintiff SHELDON FONG;
Maxine Fasulis of French & Lyon (415.597.7817) attorney for Defendant EAST WEST BANK

ARGUED: Defendant EAST WEST BANK'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions And For Monetary Sanction For Willful Failure To Comply With Discovery Order And To Continue Trial Date And Summary Judgment Motion Filing And Notice Deadlines.

R U L I N G - The Court confirms the following tentative ruling as follows: Defendant's motion for terminating sanctions or in the alternative, for dismissal of the complaint, is denied in light of plaintiff's service of amended responses to defendant's special interrogatories. Defendant's alternative motion for an order deeming admitted the requests for admission that were the subject of the discovery order entered on August 14, 2014, is denied without prejudice because the requests for admission that are a subject of that order were not included in defendant's motion. Defendant's motion for monetary sanctions is granted in part. The court orders plaintiff to pay sanctions in the amount of $1,300 to defendant within 10 days of notice of entry of this order. Defendant's motion to continue the trial date and summary judgment motion filing and notice deadlines must be directed to the Presiding Judge in accordance with Local Rule 6.0.

Plaintiff's counsel shall submit an order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
Judge Pro Tem: Margaret M. Niver; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)

Court tenders its tentative ruling in open court as follows: DEFENDANT EAST WEST BANK's DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part.
